finding: May be associated with: any severe ocular malformation / optic dysplasia / trisomy 13

differentials.name: unilateral leukokoria (2nd most common cause) ◊  19-28% of leukokoria cases
differentials.raw: unilateral leukokoria (2nd most common cause) ◊  19-28% of leukokoria cases

differentials.name: ± cataract, strabismus, painful glaucoma, hyphema, uveitis
differentials.raw: ± cataract, strabismus, painful glaucoma, hyphema, uveitis

differentials.name: ophthalmoscopy: S-shaped tubular mass extending between posterior surface of lens + region of optic nerve head; lens opacity may preclude diagnosis
differentials.raw: ophthalmoscopy: S-shaped tubular mass extending between posterior surface of lens + region of optic nerve head; lens opacity may preclude diagnosis

differentials.name: Retinal detachment from organizing hemorrhage / traction
differentials.raw: Retinal detachment from organizing hemorrhage / traction

differentials.name: Hydrops / atrophy of globe + resorption of lens
differentials.raw: Hydrops / atrophy of globe + resorption of lens

differentials.name: Phthisis bulbi (scarred shrunken eye) Rx:   preservation of globe for cosmetic reasons; enucleation for retinal hemorrhage + glaucoma only
differentials.raw: Phthisis bulbi (scarred shrunken eye) Rx:   preservation of globe for cosmetic reasons; enucleation for retinal hemorrhage + glaucoma only

context: Age:   noticed at birth / within first few weeks of life Location:   unilateral ◊  Bilaterality is a feature of a congenital syndrome (Norrie disease, Warburg disease)! √  microphthalmia = small hypoplastic globe (61-92%): √  normal-sized globe (13%) √  buphthalmos (in up to 26%) √  retinal detachment ← vitreoretinal traction in 30% US: √  hyperechoic band extending from posterior pole of globe to posterior surface of lens (= embryonic rest of primary vitreus) √  central anechoic line (= persistent hyaloid artery) with positive Doppler signals √  hyperechoic band extending from papilla to ora
